bullets.text: Q2 CR 96.5 (+11 pts YOY as pandemic effects reversed); Personal Lines CR 96.2; June CR hit 100.5 — first month above 100 since Hurricane Harvey August 2017; YTD CR 93 despite monthly deterioration.

Companywide Q2 NPW up 13%; Commercial Lines Q2 NPW up 66% (partly from Protective acquisition and easy 2020 comp); Personal Lines NPW up 6%; multi-product household growth up 18% YOY, outpacing monoline growth.

Companywide PIF growth 11% YOY; Robinson (auto+home bundle) customers growing strongly — Direct Robinson PIFs up ~20% YOY; 21% of customers had multiple Progressive products; new website self-service platform launched for all eligible personal auto policyholders.

Personal auto accident frequency up 47% YOY (vs. -39% in Q2 2020) as pandemic restrictions lifted; VMTs up 20%; used vehicle valuations rising drove higher collision severity; property CR 116.6 with 17 weather events adding 25 CR points.

Rate increases effective in 11 states averaging ~5% in Q2; aggregate personal auto rate change ~2% for quarter; underwriting criteria tightened in loss-inadequate segments; media budget reduced as pricing catch-up takes priority over growth.

Q2 investment return 1.7% (strong fixed income and equity); duration maintained; credit allocation reduced on valuation concerns; capital position described as strong heading into H2 amid rising loss costs.

Auto model 8.7 in 10 states at quarter end (8.6 in 22 states); commercial model 8.0 with new policy admin system in 15 states; BOP expanded to 22 states (43% of CL multiperil market); Protective Insurance acquisition completed June 1.

Usage-based insurance data showed VMTs and congestion fully back to pre-pandemic levels; supply chain disruptions driving up total loss and repair costs; trucking benefiting from freight market strength and intermodal bottlenecks.

Plans announced to hire up to 6,400 employees through year-end 2021, including ~5,900 contact center and claims roles; Inclusion Week 2021 focused on unlocking power of allyship; CDC COVID restrictions created ongoing need for workplace flexibility.

Digital tools deployed to CRM consultants to identify cross-sell propensity for additional protection products; real-time data feeds integrated between auto and home; simplified single-credential login across Progressive auto and home online platforms.
